title: penstemon jamesii subsp. ophianthus
intro: Penstemon jamesii subsp. ophianthus, also known as the "Gypsum Beardtongue", is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be utilized to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The Gypsum Beardtongue is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial infections, and promoting tissue repair. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
